codeReview.summary: Permission tier system exists across multiple files but lacks a dedicated permission_tiers.rs module. The core enforcement logic has a critical flaw: PermissionTier::Ask is treated identically to Deny - both result in blocking execution. The system does not implement the confirmation flow that 'Ask' tier requires.

issues.description: PermissionChecker::is_allowed() only permits execution when tier is Auto. Ask tier is effectively treated as Deny since is_allowed returns false for Ask. The feature description says 'Ask confirmation for medium' but no confirmation mechanism exists - tools with Ask tier simply fail.

LocalExecutor execute() rejects any tool that is not Auto or Ask tier. This means tools with Ask tier will be passed to execute() but will then be rejected if they don't have Auto permission. There's no confirmation workflow implemented.

No dedicated permission_tiers.rs file exists. The permission tier system is distributed: swell-core/src/types.rs defines types, tools.rs implements on each tool, executor.rs has PermissionChecker, and hybrid.rs has LocalExecutor checks. This分散 makes the system harder to understand and maintain.
ShellTool has PermissionTier::Deny which correctly blocks execution. However, the system has no mechanism for explicit override to allow Deny-tier tools when needed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature specifies skillName='rust-worker' but this skill does not exist in .factory/skills/. The transcript shows the worker discovered this and used 'mission-worker-base' instead, then verified existing code. The skillFeedback.deviations notes this mismatch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: feature.skillName='rust-worker' but available skills include 'rust', 'rust-patterns', etc. Transcript shows: 'The skill rust-worker does not exist in the available skills.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed procedure=false per skillFeedback. The deviation was justified (skill doesn't exist), but this indicates features.json skillName references need validation before worker assignment.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false with deviation explanation that rust-worker skill doesn't exist

sharedStateObservations.observation: Handoff claims implementation is 'fully implemented' but the verification steps (cargo build, cargo test) don't test the Ask confirmation flow specifically. Tests pass but Ask tier behavior was not exercised.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands were generic build/test, not specific to Ask tier behavior. No test verifies that Ask-tier tools are handled differently from Deny-tier.

summary: Review FAIL: Permission tier system has critical gap - Ask tier provides no confirmation mechanism, effectively treated as Deny. No dedicated permission_tiers.rs module exists (system is distributed). Build passes but Ask-tier tools are blocked, not confirmed. Skill mismatch: feature references non-existent 'rust-worker' skill.
